title: Global Economic Growth
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
Global economic growth has shown signs of resilience in the past few years, supported by easing inflationary pressures and steady, albeit uneven, recovery in key regions, according to the OECD. After a slowerthan-expected start to 2024, global growth remains moderate, with forecasts pointing to a slight pickup in the coming quarters. OECD projects that this resilience will continue, with global GDP increasing by 3.2 percent this year and 3.3 percent in 2025 and 2026, and inflation falling towards central bank targets.
